sentences.sentence: We have long lived in an age where powerful images, catchy soundbites and too-good-to miss offers to bombard us from every quarter.
sentences.translation: 长期以来，我们生活在一个强势图像、抓耳口号和令人难以错过的优惠从四面八方轰炸我们的时代。

structure.text: We have long lived in an age
where powerful images, catchy soundbites and too-good-to miss offers to bombard us from every quarter

difficult_points: where 引导定语从句修饰 age。
offers to bombard 中 to 属源文多余成分，且 too-good-to miss 缺连字符，均按原文保留。

sentences.sentence: All around us the persuaders are at work.
sentences.translation: 在我们周围，说服者无处不在地发挥作用。

structure.text: All around us
the persuaders
are at work

difficult_points: All around us 前置以突出无处不在。
be at work 表示正在起作用，并非只指上班。

sentences.sentence: Occasionally their methods are unsubtle –the planting kiss on a baby's head by a wannabe political leader, or a liquidation sale in a shop that has been "closing down" for well over a year, but generally the persuaders know what they are about and are highly capable.
sentences.translation: 有时他们的手法并不含蓄——例如有抱负的政治人物亲吻婴儿头顶，或一家“停业”一年多的商店仍在举办清仓甩卖；但通常说服者很清楚自己在做什么，也相当能干。

structure.text: Occasionally their methods are unsubtle
the planting kiss on a baby's head by a wannabe political leader, or a liquidation sale in a shop that has been "closing down" for well over a year
but generally the persuaders know what they are about and are highly capable

difficult_points: 破折号后列举 unsubtle methods，that 修饰 shop。
what they are about 是 know 的宾语从句；planting kiss 是源文异常搭配。

sentences.sentence: Be they politicians, supermarket chains, salespeople or advertisers, they know exactly what to do to sell us their images, ideas or produce.
sentences.translation: 无论他们是政治人物、连锁超市、销售人员还是广告商，都完全知道该如何向我们推销形象、理念或产品。

structure.text: Be they politicians, supermarket chains, salespeople or advertisers
they know exactly
what to do to sell us their images, ideas or produce

difficult_points: Be they... 是省略 whether 的倒装让步结构。
what to do 是“疑问词＋不定式”，后一个 to do 表目的。

sentences.sentence: When it comes to persuasion, these giants rule supreme.
sentences.translation: 谈到说服，这些巨头占据绝对主导地位。

structure.text: When it comes to persuasion
these giants
rule supreme

difficult_points: When it comes to 是引入话题的固定结构。
rule supreme 表示拥有最高支配地位。

sentences.sentence: They employ the most skilled image-makers and use the best psychological tricks to guarantee that even the most cautious among us are open to manipulation.
sentences.translation: 他们雇用最出色的形象塑造者，运用最有效的心理技巧，以确保即使最谨慎的人也容易受到操控。

structure.text: They employ the most skilled image-makers and use the best psychological tricks
to guarantee
that even the most cautious among us are open to manipulation

difficult_points: employ 与 use 是并列谓语。
to guarantee 表目的，that 从句是 guarantee 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: We spend more time in them than we mean to, we buy 75 percent of our food from them and end up with products that we did not realize we wanted.
sentences.translation: 我们在超市里待的时间比原计划更长，75%的食物从那里购买，最后还带回一些原本没意识到自己想要的商品。

structure.text: We spend more time in them than we mean to
we buy 75 percent of our food from them
and end up with products that we did not realize we wanted

difficult_points: them 指 supermarkets，多个独立分句以逗号连接是源文拼接。
that 修饰 products，we wanted 是 realize 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: Right from the start, supermarkets have been ahead of the game.
sentences.translation: 从一开始，超市就一直领先一步。

structure.text: Right from the start
supermarkets
have been ahead of the game

difficult_points: 现在完成时强调领先状态从过去延续至今。
ahead of the game 是习语，指比竞争者更有预见性。

sentences.sentence: For example, when Sainsbury introduced shopping baskets into its 1950s stores, it was a stroke of marketing genius.
sentences.translation: 例如，森宝利在20世纪50年代的门店引入购物篮，是一项营销天才之举。

structure.text: For example
when Sainsbury introduced shopping baskets into its 1950s stores
it was a stroke of marketing genius

difficult_points: when 从句描述引入购物篮这一事件。
it 指整个引入行为；a stroke of genius 指灵光一现的妙招。

sentences.sentence: Now shoppers could browse and pick up items they previously would have ignored.
sentences.translation: 这样，顾客便能闲逛选购，并拿起以前可能会忽略的商品。

structure.text: Now shoppers
could browse and pick up items
they previously would have ignored

difficult_points: browse 与 pick up 共用 could。
they previously... 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 items。

sentences.sentence: Soon after came trolleys, and just as new roads attract more traffic, the same applied to trolley space.
sentences.translation: 不久后手推车出现了；正如新道路会吸引更多车流，手推车更大的空间也带来了更多购买。

structure.text: Soon after came trolleys
just as new roads attract more traffic
the same applied to trolley space

difficult_points: Soon after 前置引起全部倒装，主语是 trolleys。
just as 引出类比，the same 指空间增加会促使使用量增加。

sentences.sentence: Pro Merlin Stone, IBM Professor of Relationship Marketing at Bristol Business School, says aisles are laid out to maximize profits.
sentences.translation: 布里斯托商学院 IBM 关系营销教授梅林·斯通表示，过道布局是为了实现利润最大化。

structure.text: Pro Merlin Stone
IBM Professor of Relationship Marketing at Bristol Business School
says aisles are laid out to maximize profits

difficult_points: 职务短语是 Merlin Stone 的同位语；Pro 可能是 Prof 的源文缩写异常。
are laid out 为被动语态，to maximize 表目的。

sentences.sentence: Stores pander to our money-rich, time-poor lifestyle.
sentences.translation: 商店迎合我们有钱却缺时间的生活方式。

structure.text: Stores
pander to
our money-rich, time-poor lifestyle

difficult_points: pander to 带有迎合需求的含义。
money-rich, time-poor 是对照式复合形容词。

expressions.meaning_zh: 有钱却缺时间的生活方式

sentences.sentence: Low turnover products —clothes and electrical goods— are stocked at the back while high—turnover items command position at the front.
sentences.translation: 衣服和电器等低周转商品被放在后部，而高周转商品则占据前部位置。

structure.text: Low turnover products —clothes and electrical goods— are stocked at the back
while high—turnover items command position at the front

difficult_points: 破折号内是 Low turnover products 的举例同位语。
while 对比两类商品的位置；不同样式破折号为源文排版。

sentences.sentence: Stone believes supermarkets work hard to "stall" us because the more time we spend in them, the more we buy.
sentences.translation: 斯通认为，超市努力拖住顾客，因为我们在里面待得越久，买得就越多。

structure.text: Stone believes
supermarkets work hard to "stall" us
because the more time we spend in them, the more we buy

difficult_points: believes 后省略 that。
the more..., the more... 表示两者同步增加。

sentences.sentence: Thus, great efforts are made to make the environment pleasant.
sentences.translation: 因此，超市会投入大量精力营造舒适环境。

structure.text: Thus
great efforts are made
to make the environment pleasant

difficult_points: are made 是被动语态。
make the environment pleasant 是宾语加形容词补足语。

sentences.sentence: Stores play music to relax us and some even pipe air from the in-store bakery around the shop.
sentences.translation: 商店播放音乐让顾客放松，有些甚至把店内面包房的香气输送到整个店铺。

structure.text: Stores play music to relax us
and some even pipe air from the in-store bakery around the shop

difficult_points: to relax us 是目的状语。
some 指 some stores；pipe 在此作动词，表示通过管道输送。

sentences.sentence: In the USA, fake aromas are sometimes used.
sentences.translation: 在美国，商店有时会使用人造香味。

structure.text: In the USA
fake aromas
are sometimes used

difficult_points: fake aromas 指人为制造的气味。
被动语态省略了使用这些香味的商店。

sentences.sentence: The smell is both the most evocative and subliminal sense.
sentences.translation: 嗅觉既最能唤起联想，也是最潜意识化的感官。

structure.text: The smell
is both the most evocative and subliminal sense

difficult_points: both... and... 连接两个形容词。
The smell 用词不自然，语义上指 sense of smell，按原文保留。

sentences.sentence: In experiments, pleasant smells are effective in increasing our spending.
sentences.translation: 实验表明，宜人的气味能有效增加我们的消费。

structure.text: In experiments
pleasant smells
are effective in increasing our spending

difficult_points: In experiments 表示结论来自实验情境。
be effective in 后接动名词。

sentences.sentence: A casino that fragranced only half its premise saw profit soar in the aroma—filled areas.
sentences.translation: 一家只给一半场所加香的赌场发现，香气覆盖区域的利润猛增。

structure.text: A casino that fragranced only half its premise
saw profit soar
in the aroma—filled areas

difficult_points: that 修饰 casino；fragrance 在此作动词。
saw profit soar 是感官动词加宾语和动词原形。

sentences.sentence: The other success story from the supermarkets' perspective is the loyalty card.
sentences.translation: 从超市角度看，另一个成功案例是会员积分卡。

structure.text: The other success story from the supermarkets' perspective
is the loyalty card

difficult_points: from... perspective 限定评价视角。
loyalty card 指记录并奖励顾客持续消费的会员卡。

sentences.sentence: Punters may assume that they are being rewarded for their fidelity, but all the while they are trading information about their shopping habits.
sentences.translation: 顾客可能以为自己的忠诚获得了奖励，但与此同时，他们其实在交出购物习惯信息。

structure.text: Punters may assume that they are being rewarded for their fidelity
but all the while they are trading information about their shopping habits

difficult_points: Punters 在此非正式指顾客。
are being rewarded 是进行时被动；all the while 表示同一期间。

sentences.sentence: Loyal shoppers could be paying 30% more by sticking to their favourite shops for essential cosmetics.
sentences.translation: 忠诚顾客若一直光顾自己偏爱的商店购买必需化妆品，可能会多付30%。

structure.text: Loyal shoppers
could be paying 30% more
by sticking to their favourite shops for essential cosmetics

difficult_points: could be paying 表示可能持续承担更高价格。
by sticking to... 说明多付钱的原因或方式。

sentences.sentence: Research has shown that 75 percent of profit comes from just 30 percent of customers.
sentences.translation: 研究表明，75%的利润仅来自30%的顾客。

structure.text: Research has shown
that 75 percent of profit comes from just 30 percent of customers

difficult_points: that 引导 shown 的宾语从句。
just 强调利润集中于少数顾客。

sentences.sentence: Ultimately, reward cards could be used to identify and better accommodate these "elite"shoppers.
sentences.translation: 最终，奖励卡可用于识别并更好地满足这些“精英”顾客。

structure.text: Ultimately
reward cards could be used
to identify and better accommodate these "elite"shoppers

difficult_points: to identify 与 accommodate 是并列目的。
"elite"shoppers 中引号后缺空格是源文排版异常。

sentences.sentence: It could also be used to make adverts more relevant to individual consumers – rather like Spielberg's futuristic thriller Minority Report, in which Tom Cruise's character is bombarded with interactive personalized ads.
sentences.translation: 它还可用于让广告更贴合个人消费者——颇像斯皮尔伯格的未来惊悚片《少数派报告》，片中汤姆·克鲁斯饰演的角色遭到互动个性化广告的狂轰滥炸。

structure.text: It could also be used to make adverts more relevant to individual consumers
rather like Spielberg's futuristic thriller Minority Report
in which Tom Cruise's character is bombarded with interactive personalized ads

difficult_points: It 指 reward card 所代表的数据系统。
make adverts more relevant 是宾语加形容词补足语；in which 修饰电影。

sentences.sentence: If this sounds far-fetched, the data-gathering revolution has already seen the introduction of radio – frequency identification – away to electronically tag products to see who is buying what, FRID means they can follow the product into people homes.
sentences.translation: 如果这听起来牵强，数据收集革命其实已经引入了射频识别——一种给商品加电子标签、了解谁在买什么的方法；FRID 意味着商家还能追踪商品进入人们家中。

structure.text: If this sounds far-fetched
the data-gathering revolution has already seen the introduction of radio – frequency identification
away to electronically tag products to see who is buying what
FRID means they can follow the product into people homes

difficult_points: a way 被误写为 away，FRID 可能应为 RFID，people homes 缺所有格，均按源文保留。
who is buying what 是 see 的宾语从句；末段与前句仅逗号连接。

sentences.sentence: No matter how savvy we think we are to their ploys, the ad industry still wins.
sentences.translation: 无论我们自认为多么看得穿他们的伎俩，广告业仍然会获胜。

structure.text: No matter how savvy we think we are to their ploys
the ad industry still wins

difficult_points: No matter how 引导让步从句。
we think 后省略 that；savvy to 表示了解、看穿。

sentences.sentence: Adverts focus on what products do or on how they make us feel.
sentences.translation: 广告关注产品能做什么，或产品让我们产生怎样的感受。

structure.text: Adverts
focus on what products do
or on how they make us feel

difficult_points: what 与 how 分别引导两个并列介词宾语从句。
make us feel 是使役结构。

sentences.sentence: Researcher Laurette Dube, in the Journal of Advertising Research, says when attitudes are base on "cognitive foundations" (logical reasoning), advertisers use informative appeals.
sentences.translation: 研究者洛蕾特·杜贝在《广告研究杂志》中指出，当态度建立在“认知基础”（逻辑推理）上时，广告商会采用信息型诉求。

structure.text: Researcher Laurette Dube, in the Journal of Advertising Research, says
when attitudes are base on "cognitive foundations" (logical reasoning)
advertisers use informative appeals

difficult_points: says 后接由 when 从句修饰的完整陈述内容。
are base on 应为 are based on，是源文词形错误。

sentences.sentence: This works for products with a little emotional draw but high functionality, such as bleach.
sentences.translation: 这种方式适合情感吸引力较小但功能性很强的产品，例如漂白剂。

structure.text: This works for products
with a little emotional draw but high functionality
such as bleach

difficult_points: This 指 informative appeals。
with 后 emotional draw 与 functionality 形成对比，such as 引例。

sentences.sentence: Where attitude is based on effect (i.e, emotions), ad teams try to tap into our feelings.
sentences.translation: 当态度建立在情感（即情绪）基础上时，广告团队会设法调动我们的感受。

structure.text: Where attitude is based on effect (i.e, emotions)
ad teams try to tap into our feelings

difficult_points: Where 在此表示“在……情况下”。
effect 很可能应为 affect，i.e, 标点也异常，按源文保留。

sentences.sentence: Researchers at the University of Florida recently concluded that our emotional responses to adverts dominate over "cognition".
sentences.translation: 佛罗里达大学的研究人员最近得出结论：我们对广告的情感反应会压倒“认知”。

structure.text: Researchers at the University of Florida recently concluded
that our emotional responses to adverts dominate over "cognition"

difficult_points: that 引导 concluded 的宾语从句。
dominate over 表示情感反应比理性认知更具支配力。

sentences.sentence: Advertisers play on our need to be safe (commercials for insurance), to belong (make a customer feel they are in the group in fashion ads) and for self – esteem (aspirational adverts).
sentences.translation: 广告商利用我们对安全感（保险广告）、归属感（时尚广告让顾客觉得自己属于某个群体）和自尊（励志型广告）的需求。

structure.text: Advertisers
play on our need
to be safe (commercials for insurance), to belong (make a customer feel they are in the group in fashion ads) and for self – esteem (aspirational adverts)

difficult_points: play on 表示利用某种心理需求。
to be safe、to belong 与 for self-esteem 形式不平行，是源文表达。

sentences.sentence: With time and space at a premium, celebrities are often used as a quick way of meeting these needs – either because the celeb epitomizes success or because they seem familiar and so make the product seem "safe".
sentences.translation: 由于时间和版面都很宝贵，名人常被用作快速满足这些需求的手段——要么因为名人代表成功，要么因为他们显得熟悉，从而让产品显得“安全”。

structure.text: With time and space at a premium
celebrities are often used as a quick way of meeting these needs
either because the celeb epitomizes success or because they seem familiar and so make the product seem "safe"

difficult_points: with 复合结构说明广告资源有限。
either because... or because... 列出使用名人的两种理由。

sentences.sentence: A survey of 4,000 campaigns found ads with celebs were 10 percent more effective than without.
sentences.translation: 一项针对4,000个广告活动的调查发现，有名人参与的广告比没有名人的广告有效10%。

structure.text: A survey of 4,000 campaigns found
ads with celebs were 10 percent more effective than without

difficult_points: found 后省略 that。
than without 后省略 celebs，形成有无名人的比较。

sentences.sentence: Humor also stimulates a rapid emotional response.
sentences.translation: 幽默也会激发迅速的情感反应。

structure.text: Humor
also stimulates
a rapid emotional response

difficult_points: also 承接名人之外的另一种情感策略。
stimulate 表示触发、激发反应。

sentences.sentence: Heiman Chung, writing in the International Journal of Advertising, found that funny ads were remembered for longer than straight ones.
sentences.translation: 钟海曼在《国际广告杂志》中撰文指出，幽默广告比严肃直白的广告更容易被长久记住。

structure.text: Heiman Chung
writing in the International Journal of Advertising
found that funny ads were remembered for longer than straight ones

difficult_points: writing... 是补充作者出处的现在分词短语。
ones 替代 ads；were remembered 是被动语态。

sentences.sentence: Combine humor with sexual imagery – as in Wonderbra's "Hello Boys" ads— and you are on to a winner.
sentences.translation: 把幽默与性感意象结合起来——如 Wonderbra 的“Hello Boys”广告——你就找到了制胜办法。

structure.text: Combine humor with sexual imagery
as in Wonderbra's "Hello Boys" ads
and you are on to a winner

difficult_points: “祈使句＋and＋陈述句”表达条件与结果。
be on to a winner 表示找到很可能成功的办法。

sentences.sentence: Slice-of-life ads are another tried and tested method—they paint a picture of life as you would like it, but still, one that feels familiar.
sentences.translation: 生活片段式广告是另一种久经考验的方法——它们描绘你向往的生活，同时又让这种生活显得熟悉。

structure.text: Slice-of-life ads are another tried and tested method
they paint a picture of life as you would like it
but still, one that feels familiar

difficult_points: 破折号后解释这种广告的运作方式。
one 指 a picture of life，that 修饰 one。

sentences.sentence: Abhilasha Mehta, in the Journal of Advertising Research, noted that the more one's self-image tallies with the brand being advertised, the stronger the commercial.
sentences.translation: 阿比拉莎·梅塔在《广告研究杂志》中指出，一个人的自我形象与广告品牌越契合，广告的说服力就越强。

structure.text: Abhilasha Mehta, in the Journal of Advertising Research, noted
that the more one's self-image tallies with the brand being advertised, the stronger the commercial

difficult_points: the more..., the stronger... 是相关比较结构。
being advertised 是进行时被动分词短语，修饰 brand。

sentences.sentence: Ad makers also use behaviorist theories, recognizing that the more sensation we receive from an object, the better we know it.
sentences.translation: 广告制作者也运用行为主义理论，认为我们从一个物体获得的感官刺激越多，就越了解它。

structure.text: Ad makers also use behaviorist theories
recognizing
that the more sensation we receive from an object, the better we know it

difficult_points: recognizing 的逻辑主语是 Ad makers。
the more..., the better... 表示刺激与熟悉程度同步增加。

sentences.sentence: If an advert for a chocolate bar fails to cause salivation, it has probably failed.
sentences.translation: 如果巧克力棒广告没能让人流口水，它大概就失败了。

structure.text: If an advert for a chocolate bar fails to cause salivation
it has probably failed

difficult_points: 两个 fail 含义不同：前者指未能引发生理反应，后者指广告整体失败。
it 指 an advert。

sentences.sentence: No wonder advertisements have been dubbed the "nervous system of the business world".
sentences.translation: 难怪广告被称为“商业世界的神经系统”。

structure.text: No wonder
advertisements have been dubbed the "nervous system of the business world"

difficult_points: No wonder 表示由前述事实自然得出的判断。
have been dubbed 是现在完成时被动，dub 后接宾语补足语。

sentences.sentence: Probably all of us could make a sale if the product was something we truly believed in, but professional salespeople are in a different league—the best of them can always sell different items to suitable customers in the best time.
sentences.translation: 如果产品是我们真正信赖的东西，或许人人都能卖出去；但专业销售员属于另一个层次——其中最优秀的人总能在最佳时机把不同商品卖给合适顾客。

structure.text: Probably all of us could make a sale if the product was something we truly believed in
but professional salespeople are in a different league
the best of them can always sell different items to suitable customers in the best time

difficult_points: we truly believed in 是省略关系代词的定语从句。
be in a different league 表示能力层次截然不同。

sentences.sentence: They do this by using very basic psychological techniques.
sentences.translation: 他们通过运用非常基础的心理技巧做到这一点。

structure.text: They
do this
by using very basic psychological techniques

difficult_points: They 指 professional salespeople，this 指成功销售。
by doing 表示实现结果的手段。

sentences.sentence: Stripped to its simplest level, selling works by heightening the buyer's perception of how much they need a product or service.
sentences.translation: 简化到最基本的层面，销售就是通过增强买家对自己多么需要某种产品或服务的感知来发挥作用。

structure.text: Stripped to its simplest level
selling works
by heightening the buyer's perception of how much they need a product or service

difficult_points: Stripped... 的逻辑主语是 selling。
how much... 是介词 of 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: Buyers normally have certain requirements by which they will judge the suitability of a product.
sentences.translation: 买家通常有一些特定要求，并会据此判断产品是否合适。

structure.text: Buyers normally have certain requirements
by which they will judge the suitability of a product

difficult_points: by which 引导定语从句，which 指 requirements。
by 表示这些要求是判断依据。

sentences.sentence: The seller, therefore, attempts to tease out what these conditions are and then explains how their products' benefit can meet these requirements.
sentences.translation: 因此，卖家会设法探明这些条件是什么，再说明产品的优势如何满足这些要求。

structure.text: The seller, therefore, attempts to tease out what these conditions are
and then explains how their products' benefit can meet these requirements

difficult_points: what 与 how 分别引导 tease out 和 explains 的宾语从句。
products' benefit 数和所有格搭配不自然，是源文表达。

sentences.sentence: Richard Hession, author of Be a Great Salesperson says it is human nature to prefer to speak rather listen, and good salespeople pander to this.
sentences.translation: 《成为优秀销售员》的作者理查德·赫申说，喜欢说而不喜欢听是人的天性，优秀销售员会迎合这一点。

structure.text: Richard Hession, author of Be a Great Salesperson says
it is human nature to prefer to speak rather listen
and good salespeople pander to this

difficult_points: author... 是 Richard 的同位语，之后原文缺逗号。
it 是形式主语；rather listen 缺 than/to，是源文省略异常。

sentences.sentence: They ask punters about their needs and offer to work with them to achieve their objectives.
sentences.translation: 他们询问顾客需求，并主动提出与顾客合作实现目标。

structure.text: They ask punters about their needs
and offer to work with them to achieve their objectives

difficult_points: They 指 good salespeople，punters 指顾客。
to work 是 offer 的宾语，to achieve 表目的。

sentences.sentence: As a result, the buyer feels they are receiving a"consultation" rather than a sales pitch.
sentences.translation: 结果，买家会觉得自己得到的是一次“咨询”，而不是推销说辞。

structure.text: As a result
the buyer feels
they are receiving a"consultation" rather than a sales pitch

difficult_points: feels 后省略 that，they 指单数 buyer 的性别中性用法。
rather than 对比 consultation 与 sales pitch；a 与引号间缺空格。

sentences.sentence: All the while, the salesperson presents with a demeanour that takes it for granted that the sale will be made.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，销售员表现出一种认定交易必然成交的姿态。

structure.text: All the while
the salesperson presents with a demeanour
that takes it for granted that the sale will be made

difficult_points: that 修饰 demeanour，但语义上应由 salesperson 执行 takes，源文搭配略生硬。
take it for granted that 中 it 是形式宾语。

sentences.sentence: Never will the words "if you buy" be used, but rather "when you buy".
sentences.translation: 绝不会使用“如果你购买”这样的说法，而会说“当你购买时”。

structure.text: Never will the words "if you buy" be used
but rather "when you buy"

difficult_points: Never 置于句首引起助动词倒装。
but rather 后省略 will the words... be used。

sentences.sentence: Dr Rob Yeung, a senior consultant at business psychologists Kiddy and Partner, says most salespeople will build up a level of rapport by asking questions about hobbies, family and lifestyle.
sentences.translation: 商业心理机构 Kiddy and Partner 的高级顾问罗布·杨博士说，大多数销售员会通过询问爱好、家庭和生活方式来建立一定程度的融洽关系。

structure.text: Dr Rob Yeung
a senior consultant at business psychologists Kiddy and Partner
says most salespeople will build up a level of rapport by asking questions about hobbies, family and lifestyle

difficult_points: 职务短语是主语同位语。
by asking... 表示建立 rapport 的手段。

sentences.sentence: This has the double benefit of making the salesperson likeable while furnishing him or her with more information about the client's wants.
sentences.translation: 这样做有双重好处：既让销售员显得讨人喜欢，又为其提供更多有关客户需求的信息。

structure.text: This has the double benefit
of making the salesperson likeable
while furnishing him or her with more information about the client's wants

difficult_points: This 指询问私人兴趣以建立关系。
making... 是宾语加形容词补足语；furnish... with... 表示提供。

sentences.sentence: Yeung says effective salespeople try as far as possible to match their style of presenting themselves to how the buyer comes across.
sentences.translation: 杨说，高效销售员会尽可能让自己的呈现方式与买家给人的印象相匹配。

structure.text: Yeung says
effective salespeople try as far as possible
to match their style of presenting themselves to how the buyer comes across

difficult_points: as far as possible 表示尽最大可能。
how the buyer comes across 是介词 to 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: If the buyer cracks jokes, the salespeople will respond in kind.
sentences.translation: 如果买家开玩笑，销售员也会以同样方式回应。

structure.text: If the buyer cracks jokes
the salespeople will respond in kind

difficult_points: If 引导真实条件从句。
in kind 表示以同类方式回应。

sentences.sentence: If the buyer wants detail, the seller provides it, if they are more interested in the feel of the product, the seller will focus on this.
sentences.translation: 如果买家想了解细节，卖家就提供细节；如果买家更在意产品感觉，卖家就会重点谈这一点。

structure.text: If the buyer wants detail
the seller provides it
if they are more interested in the feel of the product
the seller will focus on this

difficult_points: 两个 if 条件结构仅用逗号连接，是源文逗号拼接。
they 指 buyer，this 指 the feel of the product。

sentences.sentence: At its most extreme, appearing empathetic can even include the salesperson attempting to"mirror" the hobby language of the buyer.
sentences.translation: 在最极端的情况下，表现同理心甚至包括销售员试图“模仿”买家的爱好用语。

structure.text: At its most extreme
appearing empathetic
can even include the salesperson attempting to"mirror" the hobby language of the buyer

difficult_points: appearing empathetic 是动名词短语作主语。
the salesperson 是 attempting 的逻辑主语；to 与引号间缺空格。

sentences.sentence: Whatever the method used, all salespeople work towards one aim: "closing the deal".
sentences.translation: 无论采用什么方法，所有销售员都朝着一个目标努力：“完成交易”。

structure.text: Whatever the method used
all salespeople work towards one aim
closing the deal

difficult_points: Whatever 引导让步结构，used 是过去分词修饰 method。
冒号后动名词短语解释 one aim。

sentences.sentence: In fact, they will be looking for "closing signals" through their dealings with potential clients.
sentences.translation: 事实上，他们会在与潜在客户打交道的过程中寻找“成交信号”。

structure.text: In fact
they will be looking for "closing signals"
through their dealings with potential clients

difficult_points: will be looking 使用将来进行时，强调持续观察。
through... 表示寻找信号的过程和渠道。

sentences.sentence: Once again the process works by assuming success.
sentences.translation: 这个过程再次通过预设成功来发挥作用。

structure.text: Once again
the process works
by assuming success

difficult_points: Once again 呼应前文销售员默认交易会成功。
by assuming... 表示这一技巧的运作方式。

sentences.sentence: The buyer is not asked "are you interested?" as this can invite a negative response.
sentences.translation: 销售员不会问买家“你感兴趣吗？”，因为这样可能招致否定回答。

structure.text: The buyer is not asked "are you interested?"
as this can invite a negative response

difficult_points: is not asked 是被动语态，引号内是问题内容。
as 引导原因，this 指直接询问是否感兴趣。

sentences.sentence: Instead, the seller takes it for granted that the deal is effectively done: when the salesman asks you for a convenient delivery date or asks what color you want, you will probably respond accordingly.
sentences.translation: 相反，卖家直接认定交易实际上已经完成：当销售员询问方便的送货日期或你想要什么颜色时，你很可能会顺势回答。

structure.text: Instead, the seller takes it for granted
that the deal is effectively done
when the salesman asks you for a convenient delivery date or asks what color you want
you will probably respond accordingly

difficult_points: it 是形式宾语，that 从句是真正宾语。
what color... 是 asks 的宾语从句；accordingly 指按成交已定的框架作答。

sentences.sentence: Only afterwards might you wonder why you proved such a pushover.
sentences.translation: 直到事后，你才可能纳闷自己为何如此容易被说服。

structure.text: Only afterwards
might you wonder
why you proved such a pushover

difficult_points: Only afterwards 置于句首引起情态动词倒装。
why 引导 wonder 的宾语从句；prove 在此表示结果显示为。
